THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DAWN V. ALLEN'S MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST HER ON NOVEMBER 7, 2008, AND THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST HER ON DECEMBER 2, 2008 IS DENIED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) ORDER SIGNED. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default And Default Judgment, Pursuant To Court?S Equitable Powers, Based On Extrinsic Mistake
Off Calendar Oct-21-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TIMOTHY F. PERRY, Esq. of Hornstein Law Offices (Tel: 415-454-1490) for plaintiff Hornstein Law Offices;
Atty. AMY S. PARK, Esq. of O'Melveny & Myers LLP (Tel: 650-473-2600) for defendant Dawn V. Allen.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DAWN ALLEN's Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default And Default Judgment, Pursuant To Court's Equitable Powers, Based On Extrinsic Mistake.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Dawn V. Allen's motion to vacate the default entered against her on November 7, 2008, and the default judgment entered against her on December 2, 2008 is denied. Defendant fails to show by a preponderance of the evidence a basis for equitable relief. (Iott v. Franklin (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 521, 528 ["The burden of establishing [mistake] is upon the party seeking relief who must prove it by a preponderance of the evidence."].)

"To qualify for equitable relief based on extrinsic mistake, the defendant must demonstrate: (1) a meritorious case; (2) a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action; and (3) diligence in seeking to set aside the default once the fraud or mistake had been discovered." (Mechling v. Asbestos Defendants (2018) 29 Cal.App.5th 1241, 1246 (citatoins and quotations omitted).) "When a default judgment has been obtained, equitable relief may be given only in exceptional circumstances." (Id.) Beyond the six-month jurisdictional period for relief in section 473, "there is a strong public policy in favor of the finality of judgments and only in exceptional circumstances should relief be granted." (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 982.)

Here, Defendant fails to demonstrate a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action. Defendant's declaration (see Allen Decl., 6-8) is rebutted by Plaintiff's detailed declaration and accompanying documentary evidence. Plaintiff presents contemporaneous invoices showing that Plaintiff met in person with Mr. Hornstein three times, made or received at least 83 phone calls on her personal cell phone to Plaintiff, verified and signed two complaints Plaintiff filed for her, and paid for Plaintiff's services with personal checks. (Hornstein Decl., 1-5, 10, 17, 19-21; Exs. E, G, H, I, J.)

Defendant also fails to show reasonable diligence in seeking to set aside the defaults, which were entered 13 years ago. Defendant's declaration (see Allen Decl., 9-10) is contradicted by Plaintiff's declarations and accompanying documentary evidence. Plaintiff presents proof of service showing that a registered process server personally served Defendant at her home at 8:15am on September 21, 2008 with the summons, complaint, ADR notice and notice of Case Management Conference. (Bartels Decl., 4-10, Ex. A.) Under California Evidence Code section 647, "[t]he return of a process server . . . upon process or notice establishes a presumption, affecting the burden of producing evidence, of the facts stated in the return." Defendant fails to rebut this presumption of service. (See Cal. Civ. Proc. Code Sec. 415.10 ["A summons may be served by personal delivery of a copy of the summons and of the complaint to the person to be served. Service of a summons in this manner is deemed complete at the time of such delivery."]; Bd. of Trustees of Leland Stanford Junior Univ. v. Ham (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 330, 336 ["Service is deemed complete at the time of delivery."].) Additionally, Plaintiff presents evidence that he served Defendant with a request for entry of default judgment and a notice of entry of default at the same address where she had been served with the summons and complaint. (Hornstein Decl., 26, 28, Exs. M, O.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2008 Dismissal Or Judgment On File As To All Defendants

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Dec-02-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Val D. Hornstein, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Hornstein Law Offices, Prof. Corp.
Val D. Hornstein, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Dawm V. Allen.
Amended Default was entered against defendant on November 7, 2008.
Doe defendants were dismissed on November 21, 2008.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Hornstein Law Offices, Prof.Corp., and against the Defaulted Defendant Dawn V. Allen as follows:

Damages/Principal - $16,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $666.00.
Costs - $365.00, for a total of $17,031.00.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
